contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 90
- Description: Partner Check-in dual-use surface + trees/mo accounting cleanup
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/247
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/248
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/121
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/122
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/75
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/76
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/131
  
  Details:
  Burst of operational work after Gary asked to (a) reuse the Partner Check-in surface for tracking Matheus + Omega Services freight follow-ups, and (b) make sure their numbers don't pollute Trees Financed/mo on truesight.me.
  
  ## Shipped (6 merged PRs)
  
  - dapp PR #247 — partner_check_in picker whitelist extends to Operator type (Matheus, partner_type=Operator, slug black-king-ilheus).
  - dapp PR #248 — picker whitelist further extends to Freight Provider (Isis Riberio @ Omega Services), AND each Partner Check-in / Partner Stock bell item now carries its own deep-link ?partner_id=<slug> so clicking a card drops the operator into the form with that partner pre-selected. Per-item link structure required restructuring the popup HTML so each row is independently clickable. Cache busted to ?v=20260512e.
  - market_research PR #121 → #122 — sync_sell_through_report.py filter for Trees Financed/mo. First version was an allow-list (Consignment + Wholesale only). Gary then clarified Kiki's Cocoa is partner_type=Operator but also handles online fulfillment (real sell-through). Pivoted to a deny-list (Freight Provider + Supplier only). Operator stays IN because their sales_monthly is populated only from actual QR Code Sales events. Known limitation: Operator inventory_units may slightly over-count Trees in Pipeline if they hold bulk-warehouse + retail stock at same location — queued in OPEN_FOLLOWUPS.
  - truesight_me_beta PR #75 → #76 — defensive JS filter on index.html matching the same deny-list, belt-and-suspenders for any stale CDN-cached sell-through-report.json.
  - agentic_ai_context PR #131 — PARTNER_CHECK_IN_IMPLEMENTATION.md §15 documents the dual-use convention: partner_type taxonomy, field semantics for non-retail rows (Method + Next Check-in Date + Notes are load-bearing; Stock/Restock cluster is dead weight), and downstream consequences for Trees/mo accounting. OPEN_FOLLOWUPS.md gets three new queued items: paste-image-as-attachment for Partner Check-in, Beer Hall daily digest integration, finer-grained inventory tag for hybrid Operators.
  
  ## Why this matters
  - The Partner Check-in surface is now the universal operator-driven follow-up cadence tracker, not just retail. Same UI, same bell, same auto-checkin-on-send.
  - Trees/mo on truesight.me stops counting upstream supply-chain entities while keeping online-fulfillment sales legitimately included.
  - The dual-use convention is captured in agentic_ai_context so future LLM sessions don't try to 'fix' the partner_type extension by reverting it.
  
  Operator next steps:
  1. Re-run market_research/scripts/sync_sell_through_report.py and commit refreshed sell-through-report.json to agroverse-inventory so the canonical filter takes effect.
  2. Re-run sync_partners_velocity.py to propagate Isis Riberio (Agroverse Partners row 42) into partners-velocity.json so she's pickable in the form.
  3. File the first check-in with Matheus on the AGL7 freight.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 150.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
